title: amblostoma micranthum
intro: Amblostoma micranthum, also known as the "small-flowered poison vine" or "Cup plant," is a plant species in the Bignonaceae family. It is a perennial vine with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant contains alkaloids and other compounds with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, making it beneficial for reducing fever and alleviating pain, as well as treating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Amblostoma micranthum include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to promote health and well-being.
